reviews.review_text:
Every lord in this game got goofy ahh decision making.
We are losing the war badly.
Cool. Let’s siege a random castle 900 miles away with no food.

Enemy nobles escape prison like it’s a part time job.
Khuzaits moving around the map like they got creative mode enabled.

I genuinely started tweaking whenever some random lord with 11 troops raided my village, ran away for 3 days, then came back and did it again.
Still somehow one of the most addicting games I’ve played.
Recommended.

The devs hate themselves, you((especially if you are a modder), me and their game. No real meaningful updates in *checks notes* ever.

You can have fun in this game but then after a while you realize basic QOL features are not implemented UNLESS you use a mod. So you decide to do that and use a mod. If it is compatible with the current version of the game GREAT! But wait until next month when the devs push a patch for some BS feature or system that no one has complained about ever. Now your mod(s) broke. I often judge a game base on the money I spend and time I get out of it. I got my moneys worth out of this game so I aint too mad. But the reason I logged so many hours is because I wasnt playing vanilla, I was playing with mods. I recommend someone buys this game its you can find it on sale. Otherwise dont waste your time. Also dont worry about all the ONE DLC they dropped since launch. Yes you read that right. 1 DLC lmaoooo. These devs suck

I love this game, it has so much potential, but honestly, the base game is so bare bones that you rely on mods to flesh it out to a playable and enjoyable point. The problem comes in with the instability of the game itself that means you spend days on modding the game and figuring out what works with what.

That frustration is multiplied a million fold because the incompetence of the developers forces constant useless updates onto the community that breaks all the modders hard work. Every single update breaks the majority of mods and most modders that have created amazing content for this game have ended up abandoning the projects and the game completely.

I am today, with the release of 1.4.5, one of the players that will now uninstall this game, and maybe check in, in another 5 years to see if there is a minute chance of playing a stable game that should have been sorted out years ago!!! 

I am honestly so tired of developers half @ssing a game and spending years after release fixing what should have worked from day one.

Dont waste your time on this now in my opinion. you are just going to waste, like me, 450 hours of your life (most of that trying to build a modlist that actually works)

Absolutely recommended!

Every so often, a game becomes synonymous with its genre. Titles like Minecraft and GTA define the standards we use to describe how other games play, and Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord is undoubtedly one of these games.

It isn't perfect—bugs can take ages to be patched, and the developers haven’t always provided the level of support the modding community deserves. However, despite this, it remains one of the very best world simulators ever made. Hopefully we get more content for this game for a long time!

Execute everyone.
Doesn't matter if you lose relations if there is no one left to have a relation with.

10/10 would recommend!!!

The concept is great and I can enjoy playing the game most of the time. But I can not recommend as it is not even near a finished product. The game is slightly better now than what it was 6 years ago when the early access started, but sadly has not gotten the development attention it would so desperately need and deserve.

Very ambitious, feature-rich, expansive, utterly ridiculous and janky. 

Campaign mode is awesome. It's like Total War from first/third-person perspective. With complex trading. And weapon forging and customization.
There is some much freedom it's hard to explain. You were luckly enough to be perceived as a noble in medieval times. The world is your oyster. Or your children's should you decide to kick the bucket.
On the topic of children, it is quite likely that if you enter a tournament in town, one of your opponents could be your dear wife. If she's pregnant at the time - it's fine. An axe to the head or pillum in the gut DOES NOT HAVE ANY EFFECT ON CHILD'S STATS.

Multiplayer is also fun. Most of the stuff is happening on RP servers though.

There are lots and lots of mods available. Content, QoL, total conversions, yet more new mechanics - it's all there. One of the most modded games in fact.

As for the problems, there is significant amount of jank. Especially around new features.
For instance, footmen AI turns off completely when they get into water that is chest-deep.
Stealth is weird. It works fine, I guess. I just can't understand why it's there in the first place. But quite likely I am just missing something. There is just so much mechanics in the game it's easy to miss some.

Anyway, the game is good and is getting better with each patch. Worth the time.

I played this a lot. I like this game. But I do not recommend this game. I think they've done a very good job with the battle mechanics, which are just kind of a fun grind to veg out on. The battles are impressive in the level of detail I can get even on my old 3080, and they are a technical accomplishment they should be proud of (even when pathing bugs and it sends your framerate to zero).

The rest of the game feels like a series of disparate and unfinished features.  I think this game is an example of the danger of scope creek, and they needed to cut the scope so they can focus on the core game to make a more complete holistic game. It's a collection of barely connected features that are mostly pointless to engage with, including the core questline. Blacksmithing, diplomacy, relationships, tournements, trading, etc, are functionally not very worth engaging with. Battle AI is pretty good, but campaign map AI is very bad. You can end up stuck whacking moles because your own kingdom seems to like sending underpowered armies and getting your entire military killed off, and then going ahead and declaring wars on new kingdoms anyway. You basically have to do it all yourself until your enemies are so thoroughly defeated that your AI can finally help a little with castle capture. 

It's shame they couldn't keep feature creep in check it could have been great.
thanks for the pointless updates that broke every mod i like i find no more enjoyment spending hours troubleshooting this game to play it the way i did before

There is Battle Brothers.
There is Wartales.

There are a lot of such games that try and do what Mount and Blade does. They all fall a little short. This game series is very special to me, even if it sometimes disappoints.

I highly recommend Mount & Blade: Bannerlord.

A basically good sandbox in which you can do and try many things. 
For me, it just became boring at some point because it simply lacks a good story, 
but this game doesn't even want to have one. 
Therefore, it is simply a good sandbox, with cool fights and battles.

Horrible stability. The more I do something, the laggi-er it becomes, no matter what fixes I tried. If you only want to spend a couple years or so in game to play around then it's fine. But if you want to unite the continent then find a different game. What a waste.

I kinda still do love this game and like to play it but the main issue for me is that its way too expensive for an unfinished game but it has SO MUCH potential and thats so frustrating it could be top 5 games of this type if just some thought was put into making it better and adding new stuff. Among other things i dislike the ranged weapon leveling just useing them is so funky dont even get me speaking about spears omg

A large world with international politics and living settlements.  You begin living on the fringes of society and must build a gang of hoodlums into a private army.  The game permits a wide range of routes to do this.   The key decision in the game is how to build that army.  What mix of infantry, archers, cavalry from what culture.  It is possible for the game to simulate battles but battles are this game's specialty.  Suprisingly intuitive field commands.  A virtuoso blend of tactics and strategy.  Highly recommended.  8/10.

I used to absolutely  love bannerlord. This game was the only reason I got into pc gaming in the first place. To put it simply, don't waste your time. War sails update turned this game into absolute trash, i cant even get a fresh install on a new computer to work. Most times you go to launch will result in a crash. If you get lucky you might get see the loading screen before you crash loading a save.

Find another rts that works, unfortunately this game was murdered by bad development to try and make more money on an underbaked dlc and no longer works. save your time and money

The base game just feels shallow. The idea behind Bannerlord is great, but the execution never really reaches its potential. A lot of the time it honestly feels more like Warband with better graphics and UI rather than a true next step for the series.

NPCs mostly feel like moving army generators instead of actual characters. There’s barely any meaningful interaction with them, no real personality, and very little reason to care about anyone in the world. Diplomacy is also extremely basic. Kingdoms are basically either at war, neutral, or allied, and you have almost no real influence over any of it. Wars start out of nowhere with a simple vote and there’s no buildup, tension, negotiation, or politics leading up to it. The world ends up feeling static and lifeless because of it.

The AI can also be incredibly dumb. During sieges, ranged troops will happily stand in completely exposed positions getting filled with arrows instead of using cover while reloading or repositioning. Stuff like that happens way too often.

The combat is still the best part of the game though. Big battles are fun, sieges can be chaotic in a good way, and when everything works properly the game can genuinely be addictive.

MODS:
Honestly, the modding community carries this game hard. A huge amount of the depth, quality of life, diplomacy improvements, AI fixes, and general polish comes from mods. The work modders have done is seriously impressive.

The problem is the developers don’t seem to have really considered how dependent the game has become on modding. Almost every update breaks something. Even after you finally get a stable mod list working, another update comes out and suddenly you’re spending hours trying to figure out which mod broke the game this time.

What makes it more frustrating is that a lot of these updates feel minor compared to the damage they do to the modding scene. Sometimes modders already fixed the issue months or years earlier, then the devs finally release their own version of the fix and it ends up breaking half the mod ecosystem again. A lot of the time it genuinely feels like the game would have been better off left alone instead of constantly destabilizing working mod setups for changes that barely affect gameplay.

I'd easily have 1000+ hours in this game if the mods that actually make the game feel complete weren’t constantly getting broken by minor updates. That’s probably the most frustrating part. The core of the game is good enough to sink hundreds of hours into, but every time the mod list finally becomes stable, another update comes along and breaks half of it again.

Love this game but getting it to actually launch is such a chore! Have spent hours trying to get this game to run after an update and getting an error, fixing it and then launching into another error!

Edit hours later: Officially giving up being able to play this game, it has been 2 hours of trouble shooting for nothing to change. Have verified game files, fresh installed, updated drivers, rebooted my computer, and tried numerous other things I've seen online and the game continues to stall on loading screens for 10+ minutes. Good thing they released an expansion for a game that doesn't run properly!

Initially was into this game, liked the idea of it. Feels like a mixture of Total War, Crusader Kings, and Dynasty Warriors. The problem for me was getting into the mid to late game, it just didn't really feel like there was much variety and it's a very unintuitive game. For example on it being unintuitive. I got bored trying to fight under the Vlandian's as it felt kind of pointless for expanding my personal domain. Like getting random castles not near each other every once and awhile, and the seemingly random internal political choices that came up. Just got annoying and I didn't really see the connection to what was going on with the options I would be given in certain situations. Like I was leading major campaign's and winning tournaments, but didn't really seem to matter much in a consistent way politically to me. So I decided to go out and create my own kingdom. The thing there, is that I needed Mercenary armies to get castles, and when I went out on my own I decided to give up my kingdoms to try to make sure the Vlandian's would be chill with me starting out. The problem I found though is that I couldn't hire any mercenaries, even after following multiple docs online, and spending 2 hours running around the map. I eventually tracked down one of the mercenaries and saw that I couldn't hire them (they didn't have a kingdom connected to them to be clear here.) I suspect I was unable to hire them as I wasn't a kingdom or part of a kingdom anymore. To me this sums up a lot of why I wouldn't recommend this game. It doesn't make sense to me it would be this complicated to hire mercenaries for a game like this or figure it out, it should be intuitive.

To maybe expand more on the problem I have with this game. It feels like they wanted to have deep roleplaying with many options for people, but none of those options are really fleshed out in a way that means anything in my opinion. Like having the option to use stealth in places seems interesting, but it doesn't really seem to mean much or feel important to defining your character. I think they could drop stealth or smithing from the game and it wouldn't really change much for people. It feels like the core gameplay should be starting out as a mercenary, then moving to be part of a kingdom, then moving to creating your own kingdom, or taking over the one you joined. That wasn't satisfying or intuitive to progress through. If the goal is just to allow RP in a medieval setting, there just isn't any real depth or variety to the roles that can be done, and it doesn't feel like it means much for your character. 

Toward the end of this game I kept comparing it to Crusader Kings in my mind, to figure what it felt to lack to me. Even though it appears to have more complex gameplay mechanics compared to CK, with you being able to fight in battle directly with companions, to me it felt like CK still has more depth to it than this game. The core aspects of being a mercenary and/or managing a Fief/Kingdom, just doesn't have a lot of depth to it for the amount of time it takes. I think mods will make this game more fun for a lot of people, but the core game gets pretty tedious and repetitive without much meat to it.
A great game, however modern updates have made it unplayable, with any decent size battle crashing.

[h1]We Blade at Mount... and Blade... Bannerlord?[/h1]

M&B2: Bannerlord is genuinely one of the most disappointing games of all time and I'm not even exaggerating. Well, maybe a little... but I'm also trying to entertain here so give me a second.

First of all. Yes. I have 150 hours in this game. Did I have fun for those 150 hours? Yeah, sure, for most of them and I'll tell you why.

I know how to get gone when the going gets boring. I genuinely cannot stand a boring game and once a game has me concentrating more on the Pyrocynical Slop Video in the second monitor than the game itself I just turn it off because I genuinely value my limited free time. 

Mount and Blade 2: Bannerlord gets boring very quickly once you've figured it out.

It does build a lot from the first game in some ways - the factions are more developed, managing settlements is more in-depth, there are more troop varieties and obviously the graphics are way better. However, there are many features missing, straight up downgraded, or features that either didn't need adding or have broken the game in such unique ways that I'm somewhat legitimately impressed. 

For a start - where are feasts? Those things were amazing for getting to know royalty and socialising your way into the ranks of an empire as a vassal in the first game and in this game they're just gone entirely. Now you have to search up a clan member within your codex, f ucking triangulate their position based on the current moon cycle and whispers of whispers from illiterate peasants that maybe the specific noble you're chasing had farted in this part of the continent within the last 6 months. It makes no sense. "Where is my wife?" "Last seen near this village like 6 hours ago" "So she could just be anywhere then?".

I will say the combat feels a lot better but when Bannerlord came out 12 years after its predecessor, I struggle to give the game points for this. So... I won't.

My genuine main critique with the game is that the middle game is the best part and then the late game takes a long way off a short pier directly over the Mariana Trench while wearing nice fashionable concrete boots. You go from constantly seizing castles, razing villages, and traumatising generations of young Battanians - to just hoping your sultan doesn't suddenly decide to randomly declare war on the faction paying him the most extortionate tribute, not only cutting off a great revenue stream but also your own head when they eventually capture you and put you to the block for being a proper ba stard to them.

You never get time to consolidate power, instead you're under the whims of a tyrannical dictator who just wishes to throw his generals at the organ grinder for no particular reason. And don't think about starting your own kingdom, the second you seize control of a city or castle every single faction within warmongering distance will come collect their dues from your dead body. Compared to Warband where you can steamroll enemies under miles of knights. 

I'd love to recommend this game because I've genuinely had some great times with it - Praise Be John Horse of House Horse - but when Taleworlds seems more occupied making paid (chronically subpar) DLCs instead of actually finishing the base game with all of the features they promised us - I cannot suggest rewarding them with money.

It's ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ impossible to get this game to work without preforming some version of black magic

> Ope, the mod that literally every mod on the workshop uses doesn't work? guess you're SOL lolz !

> Oh ? you've finally got it to work? well, congrats ! now you're stuck on a black screen that never goes away even when you've disabled all the mods. How do you fix it ? ooooooohhhh well, you have to open the game and change it into windowed/borderless mode ! oh right you can, guess you're SOL Lolz ! Thanks for the 50 bucks !!

Ever since this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ DLC came out it has been problem after problem with this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game. From random crashes top my game being bricked. I cannot load a save or create a new one, it opens the loading screen and stays there for hours. it could load the save just fine and play for hours and then when I quit and open back up later that day it suddenly refuses to load or work in any meaning of the word. I would say its a great game but how great can it be when an update literally breaks the game and makes it unplayable. ♥♥♥♥ Taleworlds do not give them anymore money such an incompetent developer. This is a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ product do not give them your hard earned money.

The base game is at best meh. Build an army, fight on horseback, take over the map. Then you start noticing how many things feel unfinished. basic stuff that shouldve been there on launch just... isn't there. not unless you mod it.

So you mod it. and suddenly the game is actually good. proper good. and then TaleWorlds drops a patch; for something nobody complained about, fixing something nobody asked for and half your mods are broken. the modders scramble to catch up, you sit there waiting, and then it happens again next month. it's a loop. an endless, exhausting loop. i genuinely spent more time managing mods than playing the actual game.

Then there's War Sails. The one DLC they've put out since launch. *One*. They locked the Nords ,one of the best factions from Warband, behind a paywall and the naval combat built around them is a disaster. they literally made Viking Conquest 1.5 but worse; Did they fire everyone who worked on it?

And even setting the bugs aside the entire map wasn't built for naval warfare. you can finish a full playthrough barely touching it. there's one city on an island in the northwest corner and that's your whole naval experience. one city. you paid for a naval DLC and 90% of the game is landlocked. on top of that it crashes. regularly. it's overpriced for what it is and what it is, is broken.

Bottom line: if you find it on a deep sale, sure, grab it, play it modded on 1.2.12 and have a decent time. full price? don't bother. and skip War Sails entirely until they fix it. which, going by TaleWorlds' track record, might just never happen.
Constantly crashes, saved games get corrupted. I love this game, but it's not stable enough to enjoy

Game could have been great. But 1) Many critical bugs remained in my last game. Didnt touch it for about a year. Now they bloat the game with a stupid dlc (50 gigs and later updates 5 gigs). 
Then moderators dont want you talking about force feeding you bs cancer dlc. They are ♥♥♥♥. They could have been great.

They have abandoned this game for the past 4 years. It was in early access and they made some updates. They released the full game and continued to make very small updates with hardly any significant changes to the quality of life of the game or major updates to gameplay. Modders have kept this game going being vanilla Bannerlords is trash and not worth playing. But Taleworlds is to invested in getting a console release to get new unknowing coffers to actually help the modders and give them modding support for the game. It's sad. Focusing more on console ports then actually fixing their game. After each update you have to wait about a month for modders to catch back up to make it worth playing again.

Sieges suck. Most battles suck. Most of the NPCs suck. Most of the gameplay sucks. There is still exactly one cool experience in this game and its riding around slashing people from horseback. It's addictive and the fact the devs hate that you want to do it is obvious from all the anti-fun systems they throw in to try and stop it. It's not challenging, its poorly crafted. A pisspot of failure.

after over 1300 hours i have suffered thru 100s of crashes no matter what mods i have on or what version i play the game crashes so much im getting ptsd every time theres a litle bit of lag idk if its my pc or the game its driving me insane

I got my money's worth out of it, but it pains me so much what TaleWords did to this game. The atrocious launch, bad patches and resilience to actually turn it around. Let us not even speak of the DLC.

Several mods are still must haves (e.g. RBM) and diplomacy is still lacking 4 years later.

I forgot by now what it's called, but what did it for me was patching out the ability to define your combat groups pre-deployment. They had a good pre-fight feature where you could choose which units were in what group, but TW decided they know better and patched it out, to never come back. As the last straw, I just couldnt play after that anymore.
Maybe I revisit it in 1-2 years, hoping the modders fixed the game (again).
Content does not equal good gameplay, and miserable implementation and incompetent AI which acts as the cornerstone of gameplay cannot be fixed by releasing a 25 dollar DLC

I have sunk a lot of time into this game between console and PC. It has some rough edges and is pretty bare bones as far as features go but there aren't any other games like it that I know of. Battles are the highlight of the game but it stalls out once your clan hits tier 5-6. The most fun is the early game trying to build wealth and influence as merc. The DLC is cool but performance wise tends to crash the game during the sea battles which are kind of underwhelming.

I have another 1,000+ hours on console and started playing on PC when I finally finished my setup. IDEK what to say other than: don't blame anyone else but yourself if you start playing in May and it's suddenly December. DISCLAIMER: APPLY FOR WORK LEAVE BEFORE EMBARKING ON THIS JOURNEY!

PS this game is 10/10, if it wasn't already obvious. Runs amazing for me as well.
game constantly crashes. have to make a new save every day. cant ever make progress.
Fun game, challenging, and plenty of mods with great community's to join.
The concept, most graphics and gameplay is great, but becomes quickly repetitive. Do not play unmodded, do not try to play the main story.
Game has been pretty much ruined and abandoned by the devs, leaving the modding community which they constantly ♥♥♥♥ over with what they call "Updates" to pick up the slack.

[b] Buttering the cheeks, buttering the butterlord. [/b]
Vanilla is nice, but also very nice mods made by the community.
10/10 would recommend.

Very fun game. A few years ago I started, it felt very incomplete. Like obvious quality of life things and certain aspects of the game youd think were about to get an update. Never changed
Was able to mask over that with mods and play the game at the potential I thought it had. 
Come back and pretty much every mod is broken, half are for the DLC version, half are not. Mostly non updated mods that seem to work for some people and not for others...
Seems like the people that made all the mods that made the game playable, have given up trying to maintain them. 
So here I am a couple years later, ready to play, only to find out things have moved significantly backwards since none of the mods that made the game playable work anymore.. I cant even get the game to start.. And with the amount of mods i have to get rid of to even start the game, I can already tell that it has gone downhill so much since when i last played that Im pretty much giving up attempting to play again.

Base game is very repeatable and good 8/10
The Modding community is what makes this game so special, you can add mods in that lets you play during the shogun dynasty or even during the height of the roman empire. 10/10 with mods

I had alot of fun with the mods WHEN they worked. The game works without the mods but tbh it feels barebones. With mods it crashes constantly. And every update breaks everything. Loved it at times but its so frustrating to the point I can't honestly recommend it.

Playing the game is so bad because the only thing you can do when your kingdom is attacked is to defend, but if you do that, they attack your villages and cities. The attacks on the cities are so boring because you sit and defend for hours, but they are moving towards a good system, just not that they can attack, retreat, and then come back, and the same thing happens again, and four minutes later it all happens again.

Fantastic game, one of the best. 

Take a look at mods. I want the ability to smith armor, for example, so I use bannercraft

Played medieval titles like all AOE parts. But nothing like this, the visuals, the control grouping, over all setting. Liking everything so far. This game is very addicting not sure how the 40 hours went by so fast I started this game like 1 week ago.

UPD: GAME IS CRASHING WAY TOO FREQUENTLY!
Extremely fun game full of different elements, from battle, to trade, to politics. very cool

I thought this game was amazing. I love the tactics needed to win all the battles, and I like the combat system. It seems simple, but it's difficult at the same time to give me a more fun and immersive experience, and I love making my own kingdom. It has almost all the aspects of a medieval game I was looking for, and thing its a really good purchase
Very fun game. Lot's of cool stuff to, I personally enjoy the tourneys.
One of my favourite games of all time. Really nice Admixture of RPG and strategy

Fun game for a few hours but it very quickly shows how hollow it is. I do enjoy it but also find it very dissapointing that it never lived up to its potential, even the modding scene doesnt add very much to the game despite many acting like mods are a must. Id recommend it on special, but wouldnt pay full price for what I think is half of a game that they never really finished and probably never will. :)
Excellent lore, battles, custimizing, and strategy what more could want from a game like this?
The game contains a lot of in game content and lore. The fighting system is well done. Controls and graphics are easy to change.

Really good game if you use mods and stuff, I'm not really supportive of nerfs in single player games but luckily we can change the version pretty easily. 

Graphics are really what you see what you get, looks good with reshade but understandable given how many troops can be fielded for the time it was released.

A worthy game of your time, except if you're going to main vlandians, no one likes you 10/10
Great game in general. Amazing modding community as well.
nice mechanics cool story and many possible characters to create , really enjoy the game !
Really great for wanting to play out "What if scenarios" or simply just having a random urge to get up and answer the ancestral call to unify the contient

peak.
play with mods if you actually wanna have fun, basegame is good but mods make it 100x better,
mods like eagle rising, shokuho, etc make the game much more enjoyable,
get the game if you like strategy games, management games, trading games, etc

The only place where you will get the experience of Bannerlord is from other Mount&Blade games, and sadly Bannerlord itself seems to have not moved far from it's predecessors.

What is 'the experience' then? I'd say Bannerlord holds itself up on 3 major gameplay 'pillars'. Large-scale combat, the freedom of a sandbox being the game itself, and diplomacy between dynamic NPC factions and persons.

The first pillar is realistically the only one bearing weight on the whole experience. It truly is impressive to share a battlefield with over 1000 troops where each one has it's own armor, weapons, and stat distributions. Each one can die permanently or push to victory and get promoted to higher and higher tiers of unit. Each one moves, attacks, and receives damage as an individual instead of many many other games where a group of units share a health-pool, sometimes grouping dozens or even hundreds of units as one on the field. The result of all this can range wildly anywhere between coordinated rows of arches firing over the backs of a shield-wall, stopping an advancing enemy army and priming them for a cavalry charge on their flank; all the way to complete chaos where every soldier on the battlefield is throwing themselves against their opposition in a massive sprawl of medieval mayhem. All while you are just 1 soldier on that shared battlefield of hundreds. Whether making a name for you and your family with just a few kills per battle, a seasoned war hero swatting away enemies by the dozen in every conflict, or an established lord controlling the field from horseback, giving the order to hold the line or run your enemies down.

The next pillar is a bit more wobbly in the way of the sandbox that is the wider world. You'll get handheld into a few starting points for building your dynasty but after that it's up to you if you want to raise you family's name into glory, run caravans between towns while watching the eb and flow of supply and demand, or embrace the life of a marauder taking anything you can while everyone else fights for kingdom and country. The problems here is that while many playstyles are possible, only ones where you endlessly go to war over land and power are fully catered to. Sure you CAN be a lowlife murdering criminal, sure you CAN be a trader and amass mountains of riches without shedding any blood; but ultimately you won't find considerable gratification outside of what you can make for yourself unless you lean into the warfare aspects of the game.

The final pillar is purely decorative and sadly holds very little weight in the Bannerlord experience. The NPCs are dull, predictable, idiotic robots who hardly ever feel at all human. They follow a strict flow-chart 90% of the time, flipping the switch between complete coward and reckless barbarian, between violent warmonger and peaceful monk, between loyal god-sworn servant and nonsensical hypocrite. It simply doesn't matter if they "have a reputation of being merciful and kind" or "devious and closefisted", they will simply attack you if you are their enemy and their flowchart says they have an advantage, or run from you full sprint if their flowchart predicts you are more powerful. Merciful NPCs will take you and your family hostage without a second thought, backstabbing NPCs will pledge their allegiance to a lord and serve them faithfully for decades, generous NPCs will throw a temper tantrum if they don't get the castle they wanted.
There is no consistency and no single NPC will ever feel like they have intentions that suit or benefit them. Each robot just does what all the other robots do.

The further you get away from the sandbox and diplomacy of Bannerlord, the closer you get to what is to be truly appreciate about the game: huge sprawling battles on desolate plains, dense forests, frosty mountains, or the battlements of a castle or town. Nothing quite matches up to the feeling of leading a stampede of cavalry against the enemy or using a bow to accurately pick off defenders from their battlements. And if you're considering Bannerlord because that particular part of the meal sounds good, then you don't need to worry about the appetizers or dessert menu bogging you down because the entrees are to die for.

The game is great, and the devs even added some new mechanics. There's now stealth(it's fairly simple, so don't expect MGS) and sea battles in the recent DLC. The game runs flawlessly(most of the time), but the sea battles definitely need some work to bring up the performance.

The game shines in early to midgame, where you go around trading, fighting bandits and working for various nobles and notables, doing some mercenary work. In the endgame, the game gets repetitive as you're effectively bound to your Kingdom and protecting and expanding it is a full-time job(gameplay-wise), so you're not doing other stuff by then. It would be nice if there were something to spice up the endgame and make it more interesting and varied.

I love Warband and the very first Mount & Blade that I got as a demo in a gaming magazine and played until the disc couldn't be read anymore. :) The series is a favourite of mine, even though there are games that I played a lot more over the years. I can see the devs' dedication, and I hope they won't stop working on the game and bring more cool stuff!

This is a great game that gives a combination of RTS, Action, and RP. Nothing else adds so many things in one place. There is a live community and mods to make it even better. Some people may complain about this or that but this game is very much like Skyrim, its a foundation to build almost anything you could want through mods. The base game itself is also great for what it is. Thanks to the Devs for the hard work.

The workshops and caravans should be integrated. The factories handle production, while the caravans sell the goods where they fetch the highest value. Otherwise, both sides will incur losses, and this way the gameplay becomes more engaging.

I've played a fair bit of Warband, and if you're looking for a more polished version of that, it is that. They could have done more, but they've done enough to make me want to put a few hundred hours into this game as well. It runs well, the battles feel like they did in the original, and its a conquest game. You can conquer the world, and thats what I came to this game to do. It does it, and it does it well.

A warning to new players would be to do your research before starting a story campaign versus a sandbox campaign. The benefits can very much be outweighed by the consequences of late game story progression, including consequences such as a [Minor spoiler] [spoiler] a forever war with a number of factions that can become -extremely- costly to buy off[/spoiler] there are ways to delay the negatives with mods once you're in, so it is very technically changeable, but it can get clunky fast, and it is relying on author's upkeep, which can be spotty. 

And hey, the modding is good on both games, but there are some mods for this game that I can't live without on the original, although I'm positive you'll find people who swear up and down the opposite way. The biggest complaint I've found from the community is that the game isn't feature relevant enough to warrant a new game versus the potential it could have been or was promised. I bought it at full price, and I dont regret it, bought the DLC, and still feel that the value was there.
Fun but early game is rough when you're a scrawny little peasant getting ganged up on by Steppe bandits

Mount & Blade has been one of my favourite games since I discovered it many years ago. It's a unique game, very immersive - the core gameplay loop feels rewarding: building a party into an army, then conquering. TaleWorlds released it in a poor state and they are poor at QOL fixes, and the DLC is not well thought through. That being said, I could never negatively review a game that I really enjoy.

If you've never played it, you should.

[i]I'll update this review as I progress. This is an honest review of my experience playing the game.[/i]

Oyunda bir çok şey eksik. Oyunu tamamlayan modlar var fakat her update te save bozuluyor ve tam anlamıyla oynamak için birçok mod yüklemeniz gerek. Oyunu geliştirmek yerine DLC çıkarıp önemli mekanikleri modlara bırakmışlar. Modlar her zaman güncellenmediği için de oyunun ortasında kalakalıyorsunuz.

[As of before most recent update] Overall, this game is a brilliant demonstration of how a strategy game can be built. It has everything I was looking for in terms of strategy and combat. I WOULD recommend this game if you're looking for a slow-paced game you're willing to put some hours into.

Now the downsides.
1. Diplomacy - Isn't at its full potential, your options are peace, alliance and war. Among a couple other minor additions. This game could offer so much more in terms of diplomacy that it fails to do, allying others doesn't even matter as they can simply unally against you and won't assist you in wars.
2. Battles - Battles are extremely repetitive with a lot of the same maps once you start fighting over and over. There's A LOT of micro managing needed during battles - you can instruct troops to "follow your Sergeants" but that is simply a death wish. There needs to be more tools and maps for battles.
3. DLC - There was so much more they could add to this game, and we were all waiting for the next big update, possible a multiplayer campaign as one of the biggest hopes. However, we were given a DLC which gives you ships. And that's it, the same game, but with ships. I will admit, the variety of ships and the DLC as a whole is good but in my opinion it should not be a DLC at all and the developer's focus should be dealing with the bugs and other HIGHLY suggested things - and not ignoring them or simply deciding to add it at a ridiculous price with half the things that were asked. 

If you're interested, I do recommend but of course take on board the downsides I have explained.

After reading alot of negative reviews based on the new DLC warsails, i have not come across any major bugs, glitches or crashes. i know there are mods for this but i wish there would already be a coop mode integrated as this game is beyond fun and would thoroughly enjoy playing with a mate.

Really underwhelming sequel to a good game

Doesn't really add much new things (aside from expected technical improvements such as better graphics or battle size) and simplifies previous mechanics way too much.

Also devs constantly do weird things where they microtune economics or some stats for a weapon in multiplayer that breaks all of the mods.

You can technically enjoy it, but it is definetly worse than Warband in it's core mechanics and overall vibe of the game
